moodPills: Crude shock is the real boss today (it hits inflation, rupee, and earnings).
INR slipped to record lows intraday; currency stress = risk-off for equities.
Volatility is elevated; expect whipsaws and false breakouts when VIX > 20.
Rotation is real: power/energy held up while autos and banks got hit.
DIIs are absorbing selling, but that does not mean the bottom is in.
hero.stance: Defensive first, selective offense only in relative-strength pockets.
hero.mainDriver: Oil up sharply + INR weakness + persistent FII selling = macro-driven risk-off day. citeturn18view0turn23news36turn14search1turn27view0

topActions: Do not chase rebounds in beaten-down autos/banks; wait for a clear base + improving crude/INR. citeturn21search0turn12search2turn23news36turn14search1

todayIn30Seconds.bottomLine: Markets are not reacting to company news; they are reacting to crude + currency + flows. Stay disciplined: defend, rotate, and only buy what is proving strength.

indiaPulse.marketType: Macro-driven risk-off (oil + INR + flows) with rotation into power/energy.
indiaPulse.breadth: Negative: 1653 advances / 2401 declines / 159 unchanged (NSE). Nifty 50: 11 advances / 39 declines. citeturn11view0
indiaPulse.fact: Benchmarks fell again (Nifty -0.95%, Sensex -1.08%) with 12/16 major sectors in the red; Auto index fell ~3.19% while Nifty Energy rose ~1.93%. Rupee hit record low intraday; crude was up sharply. citeturn18view0turn21search0turn23search0turn23news36turn14search1
indiaPulse.interpretation: This is a macro shock regime: when crude spikes and INR weakens, markets reprice growth and inflation risk fast. Stock-picking alpha reduces; sector selection and risk management matter more.
indiaPulse.view: Treat dips as tactical, not as 'value'. Prefer relative strength (energy/power; selective defensives) and keep autos/banks on watch until crude and INR cool.

regime.label: Risk-off correction
regime.fact: India VIX is above 20 (elevated fear). FIIs were net sellers while DIIs were net buyers. Oil up ~9% on the day; rupee hit record lows intraday. citeturn10search8turn27view0turn14search1turn23news36
regime.interpretation: When volatility is elevated and the driver is macro (oil/FX), price moves become headline-driven and correlation increases (many stocks fall together).
regime.view: Operate with a 'capital protection' mindset: fewer trades, smaller size, faster risk cuts. Deploy long-term cash gradually only into proven quality or relative strength.
whatWorks: Relative-strength sectors (energy/power) and companies with pricing power.
Staggered buying (tranches) rather than lump-sum entries.
Hedges or partial de-risking (cash is a position).
whatFails: Averaging down fast in weak sectors (autos, private banks, rate-sensitive names).
High leverage and tight-margin businesses when crude is surging.
regime.sizingNote: If you normally risk 1R per trade, cut to ~0.5–0.7R while India VIX stays >20. Keep stops hard and pre-decided (no emotional widening). citeturn10search8

avoidForSwing: High-beta mid/small names with poor liquidity
gradualAccumulationCandidates: Energy/Power on pullbacks (do not chase gaps)

sectorRotation.fact: Sector tape shows clear rotation: Energy index up ~1.93% while Auto fell ~3.19%; banks also weakened. Oil spike remains the central macro variable. citeturn23search0turn21search0turn12search2turn14search1turn18view0
sectorRotation.interpretation: This is not broad 'buy the dip'. Money is hiding in perceived beneficiaries (power/energy) and avoiding oil-sensitive cyclicals (auto) and FII-heavy segments (private banks).
sectorRotation.view: Follow the tape: build watchlists in leaders, avoid laggards, and size down until volatility cools.

breadthInternalStrength.sectorBreadth: Weak: 12 out of 16 major sectors declined.
breadthInternalStrength.defensivesLeading: No (FMCG -1.77%, Pharma -0.31%); 'defensive' leadership came mainly from energy/power today.
breadthInternalStrength.cyclicalsWeak: Yes: Auto -3.19%, Banks -1.14% (rate- and oil-sensitive cyclicals weak).
breadthInternalStrength.buyingType: Rotation + selective dip-buying (DIIs) rather than broad risk-on accumulation.
breadthInternalStrength.summary: Internal strength is weak but not capitulation: losers dominate, volatility elevated, and leadership is narrow (energy/power). citeturn11view0turn18view0turn12search1turn21search2turn23search0turn10search8turn27view0

swingRadar.setupQuality: Medium-low (high volatility + macro headlines = lower reliability).
swingRadar.fact: Energy/power showed relative strength while Auto and Financials broke down further. citeturn23search0turn21search0turn12search2turn18view0
swingRadar.interpretation: In risk-off correction regimes, the best swing ideas are usually either (1) relative strength leaders on pullbacks, or (2) clean mean-reversion bounces after capitulation. Today looks more like (1).
swingRadar.view: Take fewer trades, prioritize liquid names, and avoid chasing gaps. If you trade, trade small and be ruthless with stops.

directStockInvestorAngle.fact: Markets are being driven by crude + FX + flows more than earnings news. citeturn14search1turn23news36turn27view0
directStockInvestorAngle.interpretation: In such regimes, stock-specific fundamentals matter, but timing and sector positioning dominate outcomes in the short-to-medium term.
directStockInvestorAngle.view: Be boring: protect downside, keep cash, and scale into strength rather than averaging into weakness.

mutualFundInvestorAngle.sip: Continue SIPs as-is (this is when SIPs work best psychologically).
mutualFundInvestorAngle.lumpsum: Avoid full lumpsum today; use STP or 3–5 tranches over coming weeks while VIX stays elevated.
mutualFundInvestorAngle.deploymentStyle: Barbell: keep a liquid buffer + deploy gradually into diversified equity funds.
mutualFundInvestorAngle.dryPowder: Keep 20–35% of fresh cash as dry powder until oil/INR volatility cools.

categories.stance: Prefer for new money if you want smoother volatility
categories.comment: Good choice when you are unsure about the regime; reduces timing risk.

categories.stance: Avoid adding fresh (unless you already have a defined thesis and limits)
categories.comment: Sector funds are volatile and can underperform for long stretches in macro shocks.

mutualFundInvestorAngle.interpretation: This is exactly the environment where disciplined SIP + phased deployment beats emotional timing.
mutualFundInvestorAngle.view: Keep investing rules-based: SIP on, lumpsum phased, and rebalance rather than react.

finalBottomLine.investorConclusion: Macro risk is high. Keep cash, avoid averaging down weak cyclicals, and accumulate only in tranches into quality/relative strength.
finalBottomLine.swingTraderConclusion: Trade less and smaller. If trading, stick to relative strength (power/energy) with hard stops. Avoid autos/banks until volatility cools.
finalBottomLine.mfConclusion: SIP continues. For new lumpsum cash, use phased deployment (STP/tranches) until crude/INR stabilizes.

archiveMeta.summary: Risk-off day driven by oil shock and INR weakness. Autos and banks sold off hard; energy/power held up. FIIs sold heavily; DIIs absorbed. VIX stayed above 20.
